name: Compiler Removal of Code to Clear Buffers
description: Sensitive data stored in memory buffers is not properly cleared because the compiler's optimizer removes the clearing code (e.g., memset) as a dead store, leaving passwords and cryptographic keys recoverable from memory.
detection_pattern: TRANSFORM[memset|bzero] node clears a STORAGE[memory_buffer] containing sensitive data, but the buffer is not subsequently read, causing compiler optimization to remove the clearing operation — requires use of volatile or platform-specific secure clear functions (SecureZeroMemory, explicit_bzero)
